authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Hagan Park Pond not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Hagan Park Pond not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Sacramento Hagan Park Pond Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take January-April & November-December

sources.title: Fishbrain — coordinates for Hagan Park Pond (no GNIS gazetteer entry exists for this park pond under Layer 6 or Layer 7 of the USGS geonames ArcGIS MapServer, confirmed by direct query; coordinate corroborated by fishcaddy.com (38.604826, -121.309022) and californiafishplants.com (38.605187, -121.308773), both within ~100m of this value and all three well inside the OSM-mapped Hagan Community Park boundary, whereas the file's previous uncited coordinate (-121.309554, 38.601277) sat ~490m south, near the park's southern edge)
sources.quotedText: Hagan Park Pond is a lake located in Sacramento County, California, United States. Its coordinates are: 38°36′20.2″N 121°18′30.5″W.

parameters.note: Hagan Park Pond is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide regulation for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide pond regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Hagan Park Pond (Region 2, Sacramento Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of January-April & November-December (this entry covers the January-April window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Hagan Park Pond (Region 2, Sacramento Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of January-April & November-December (this entry covers the November-December window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
